response_body_utf8: {"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2025,9,2]],"date-time":"2025-09-02T10:55:29Z","timestamp":1756810529913},"reference-count":20,"publisher":"Wiley","issue":"6","license":[{"start":{"date-parts":[[2020,8,6]],"date-time":"2020-08-06T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1596672000000},"content-version":"vor","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"http:\/\/onlinelibrary.wiley.com\/termsAndConditions#vor"}],"content-domain":{"domain":["onlinelibrary.wiley.com"],"crossmark-restriction":true},"short-container-title":["Photoderm Photoimm Photomed"],"published-print":{"date-parts":[[2020,11]]},"abstract":"<jats:title>Abstract<\/jats:title><jats:sec><jats:title>Background<\/jats:title><jats:p>Determination of the Minimal Erythema Dose (MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub>) of unprotected skin is perhaps the most critical aspect of the Sun Protection Factor (SPF) value and disproportionately influences the SPF. Individual Typology Angle (ITA\u00b0), a skin reflectance measure of the skin's pigmentation, has been used by many clinical laboratories to estimate the MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> values for SPF testing.<\/jats:p><\/jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>Methods<\/jats:title><jats:p>Individual Typology Angle and unprotected MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> data on 9931 individuals were received from 12 laboratories located on 5 continents to determine MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> values in prospective sunscreen testing studies.<\/jats:p><\/jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>Results<\/jats:title><jats:p>These data were compiled and normalized by laboratory (to account for differences in number of subjects within each laboratory). A polynomial regression best fits the relationship between ITA\u00b0 and their MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> and may be used to accurately predict the MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> of an unknown test subject.<\/jats:p><\/jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>Conclusion<\/jats:title><jats:p>The regression data have been incorporated into the latest ISO24444 (Cosmetics \u2013 Protection test methods \u2013 in vivo determination of the sun protection factor (SPF), 2019) in vivo Sunscreen testing method that must be utilized to determine a subject's provisional MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> for the testing procedure. Use of this common ITA\u00b0\u2010MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> relationship will help to minimize SPF variability between testing laboratories due to use of widely varying MED<jats:sub>u<\/jats:sub> values.<\/jats:p><\/jats:sec>","DOI":"10.1111\/phpp.12592","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2020,7,12]],"date-time":"2020-07-12T13:50:54Z","timestamp":1594561854000},"page":"452-459","update-policy":"http:\/\/dx.doi.org\/10.1002\/crossmark_policy","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":11,"title":["Global data of unprotected skin minimal erythema dose relationship to Individual Typology Angle"],"prefix":"10.1111","volume":"36","author":[{"given":"Curtis","family":"Cole","sequence":"first","affiliation":[{"name":"Sun & Skin Consulting New Holland PA USA"}],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"311","published-online":{"date-parts":[[2020,8,6]]},"reference":[{"issue":"166","key":"e_1_2_8_2_1","first-page":"38206","article-title":"Sunscreen drug products for over\u2010the\u2010counter human use","volume":"43","author":"Labeling and Effectiveness Testing","year":"1978","journal-title":"Fed Reg"},{"key":"e_1_2_8_3_1","unstructured":"Consumer Reports.
